author: somnergy
content:
We have support for bal-devnet-7 with working SP1 hypercube prover with zilkworm as a ready docker image. The code is mostly based on the public zilkworm's alpha-2 release and bits and pieces of Amsterdam PRs curated.

To prove blocks readily use the following template
```
docker run -d --name z6m_prover_bal_devnet_7 --gpus all --rm --network host \
    -v "$PWD:/work:rw" \
    -w /work \
    -e SP1_PROVER=cuda \
    -e RUST_LOG=debug \
    somnergy/z6m_prover:bal-devnet-7   \
    --data-dir /work \
    --service \
    --rpc-url http://localhost:8545 \
    --prove-every 10
```
This is a single GPU prover (as I don't yet have sp1-cluster on this). For small gas blocks it should prove in 10 seconds or so, but for a 200M block it could take more than a minute or even more than 2 minutes. You can adust --prove-every for live proving.

There is also execution-only path, with `--exeucte-every 1`   option that will execute every block and print the cycles and prover_gas.
cc <@944007601400741989> <@427491045308235776> <@425274498732916736> <@745384946310578317>

author: somnergy
content: Regarding the code itself: It's fragmented across our internal repo, upstream evmone and probably other places as well. We will merge code in PRs to keep account of the changes. The whole thing should be available in future release. As I understand there are still some changes still to come for Amsterdam.
